ingredients.name: Lake Biwa senmatsu
ingredients.address: Lake Biwa, Shiga, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Japan's largest lake serves as the sweeping backdrop for the entire series, with its vast shoreline visible in episode 4 when Yuuta and Rikka spend time along the water. The Otsu shore of Lake Biwa frames the daily life of the characters throughout the show.

ingredients.name: Michigan Sightseeing Boat
ingredients.entityContext: The famous Lake Biwa paddle steamer Michigan is featured in the opening sequence, with a shot from its upper deck looking out over the water. The Michigan cruise from Otsu Port is one of the most recognizable pilgrimage locations tied to the series.

ingredients.name: Otsuminato Symbol Ryokuchi Park
ingredients.localName: 大津港シンボル緑地公園
ingredients.address: Otsuminato Symbol Ryokuchi Park, 5 Chome-1-7 Hamaotsu, Otsu, Shiga 520-0047, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This lakefront park beside Otsu Port is featured in the opening sequence, with scenes showing the park's promenade and the view toward Lake Biwa where the Michigan paddle steamer departs. It is part of the Hamaotsu area that anchors the show's Otsu setting.

ingredients.name: Karahashi Park
ingredients.address: Karahashi Park, 1 Chome-27 Seta, Otsu, Shiga 520-2134, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This riverside park along the Seta River near the historic Seta Karahashi bridge appears in the opening sequence and is part of the Seta area that forms the heart of the anime's Otsu setting. The park's lakeside road and riverside scenes are referenced throughout the series.

ingredients.name: Lake Biwa senmatsu
ingredients.address: Lake Biwa First Canal, 7 Miideracho, Otsu, Shiga 520-0034, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Lake Biwa First Canal at Miideracho in Otsu, a Meiji-era waterway running from Lake Biwa toward Kyoto, appears in the opening sequence as part of the waterfront scenery that defines the series' Otsu backdrop.

ingredients.name: Keihan-Ishiyama Station
ingredients.address: Keihan-Ishiyama Station, 2 Awazuchō, Otsu, Shiga 520-0832, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This Keihan Ishiyama-Sakamoto Line station is the central transit hub in the anime, appearing in the opening sequence with its distinctive level crossing. Yuuta and Rikka's school commute passes through this station, and the surrounding Ishiyama area near the Seta River is where much of the show's daily life takes place.

ingredients.name: Anoo Station
ingredients.address: Anoo Station, 2-chōme-8 Anou, Otsu, Shiga 520-0114, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This small unmanned station on the Keihan Ishiyama-Sakamoto Line is a confirmed pilgrimage location for the series, appearing early in the show as part of the characters' commute along the Keihan Otsu Line. Its compact platform and surroundings are faithfully recreated in the animation.

ingredients.name: Seta fishing port
ingredients.entityContext: This small fishing port on the Seta River near Lake Biwa appears in the opening sequence as part of the waterfront scenery that defines Rikka and Yuuta's Otsu neighborhood.

ingredients.name: Houseboat Shiki
ingredients.entityContext: This houseboat on the Seta River near Lake Biwa is a recurring pilgrimage location for the series, appearing prominently in episode 11 during a memorable scene between the characters at the water's edge.

ingredients.name: Kaigake Elementary School
ingredients.localName: しゃくなげ学校(旧鎌掛小学校)
ingredients.address: Kaigake Elementary School, 2362 Kaigake, Hino, Gamo District, Shiga 529-1631, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This 1930-built former elementary school in Hino Town, closed in 2001, is the real-world model for Sakuragaoka Academy, the high school where Yuuta and Rikka attend class. It is the single most iconic pilgrimage destination for fans of the series, with its distinctive wooden corridors and schoolyard appearing from episode 1 onward.

ingredients.name: Otsushi Shimin Bufujimi City Center
ingredients.localName: 大津市 市民部富士見市民センター
ingredients.address: Otsushi Shimin Bufujimi City Center, 富士見市民センター 2 Chome-15-33 Sonoyama, Otsu, Shiga 520-0842, Japan

ingredients.name: Hiyama Shrine
ingredients.address: Arayayama Shrine, 4-chome-2-2 Araya, Fujiyoshida, Yamanashi 403-0006, Japan

ingredients.name: Higashi-Mihama Station
ingredients.address: Higashi-Mihama Station, Ōta, Mihama, Mikata District, Fukui 919-1206, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This rural station in Mihama, Fukui Prefecture is the arrival point in episode 7, when the group travels to visit Rikka's grandparents and heads to the beach. The station became such a beloved pilgrimage destination that fans maintained an exchange notebook inside its unmanned station building.

ingredients.name: Kitada(Bus)
ingredients.address: Kitada, 570-3 Kitafudodocho, Shimogyo Ward, Kyoto, 600-8233, Japan

ingredients.name: Site of Iwadeyama Fort
ingredients.address: Site of Iwadeyama Castle, Shiroyama Iwadeyama, Ōsaki, Miyagi 989-6437, Japan

ingredients.name: Kugushi-higashi(Bus)
ingredients.address: Higashi-Furuichi, Higashifuruichi, Eiheiji, Yoshida District, Fukui 910-1212, Japan

ingredients.name: FamilyMart Tamanoura
ingredients.localName: ファミリーマート 福山野上町店
ingredients.address: FamilyMart; Fukuyama Nogamicho, 2 Chome-7-4 Nogamicho, Fukuyama, Hiroshima 720-0815, Japan

ingredients.name: FamilyMart Mihama Sata
ingredients.localName: ファミリーマート 美浜佐田店
ingredients.entityContext: This FamilyMart convenience store near Mihama in Fukui Prefecture appears in episode 8, close to the beach area featured during the group's summer trip in the Fukui/Mihama arc.

ingredients.name: Yamabiko Park
ingredients.address: Yamabiko Park, 1 Chome-1-39 Seishincho, Edogawa City, Tokyo 134-0087, Japan

ingredients.name: McDonald's Ishiyama Station
ingredients.localName: マクドナルド 石山駅前店
ingredients.address: McDonald's Ishiyama Station, 9-1 Awazucho, Otsu, Shiga 520-0832, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The McDonald's in front of Ishiyama Station is referenced in pilgrimage guides as the model for the 'EcDonald' that appears in episode 10, where characters are seen eating there.

ingredients.name: Houseboat Shiki
ingredients.entityContext: This houseboat mooring on the Seta River appears again in episode 11, part of a recurring waterside location used in emotionally pivotal scenes between Yuuta and Rikka.

ingredients.name: Coco's Seta Karahashi
ingredients.localName: ココス 瀬田唐橋店
ingredients.address: Coco's Seta Karahashi, 9-9 Karahashicho, Otsu, Shiga 520-0851, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This family restaurant near the Seta Karahashi area in Otsu appears in episode 10 as a location where the characters gather, part of the Seta neighborhood that forms the core of the anime's setting.

ingredients.name: UR Momoyama Minami
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒612-8006 Kyoto, Fushimi Ward, Momoyamacho Oshima, UR 桃山南団地 ２１

ingredients.name: Komeda's Coffee Shimokitazawa
ingredients.localName: コメダ珈琲店 下北沢店
ingredients.address: Komeda's Coffee Shimokitazawa, 浜銀下北沢ビル, ２階, 2 Chome-25-16 Kitazawa, Setagaya City, Tokyo 155-0031, Japan

ingredients.name: Houseboat Shiki
ingredients.entityContext: The Houseboat Shiki on the Seta River features in episode 11 as a waterside location that fans visit to recreate scenes from the series. This is one of three angle-matched shots of the same vessel documented by pilgrimage guides.

ingredients.name: Aming Otsu Seta
ingredients.localName: アミング 大津瀬田店
ingredients.address: Aming Otsu Seta, 1 Chome-30-1000 Seta, Otsu, Shiga 520-2134, Japan

ingredients.name: Tenno Bridge
ingredients.address: Tenno Bridge, Kawakamichō Kawanazu, Yawatahama, Ehime 796-8050, Japan

ingredients.name: Hamaotsu Skycross
ingredients.address: Hamaotsu Skycross, 1 Chome-3 Hamaotsu, Otsu, Shiga 520-0047, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Hamaotsu Skycross pedestrian bridge in Otsu, which crosses above the Keihan tram tracks near Hamaotsu station, is a confirmed pilgrimage location for the series appearing in a special episode. It is a distinctive landmark in the Hamaotsu lakefront area.

ingredients.name: Michigan Cruise (Otsu Port / Lake Biwa Kisen)
ingredients.localName: ミシガンクルーズ(大津港／琵琶湖汽船）
ingredients.address: Michigan Cruise (Otsu Port / Lake Biwa Kisen), 5 Chome-1-1 Hamaotsu, Otsu, Shiga 520-0047, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Michigan paddle steamer departs from Otsu Port and is featured in the anime, with fans able to ride the same cruise across Lake Biwa that the characters take. It is one of the most beloved pilgrimage experiences tied to the series, recommended in the official Shiga tourism guides for anime fans.

ingredients.name: Kugushi-higashi(Bus)
ingredients.address: Higashi Kusatsu, 3-chōme-4 Ōji, Kusatsu, Shiga 525-0032, Japan
